TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: Every effort must be made to increase the number of institutions capable of providing training for technical and scientific executives, whether these are secondary education institutions or higher education establishments; efforts must also be made to raise the number of available places in existing institutions, while ensuring that this increase does not lead to a lowering of academic standards.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph explicitly mentions training for technical and scientific executives, aligning with 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. The reference to academic standards and institutional capacity supports 'General (academic)'. 'Combined general and vocational' is excluded as the focus is not explicitly on integration but on expanding capacity in both sectors.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes increasing institutional capacity and maintaining academic standards, which aligns with government policy-making responsibilities. 'Teachers and educators' are directly referenced through the need for training technical and scientific executives. Other categories like 'Private sector and industry' or 'Civil society and NGOs' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Learners and students' are not the focus here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on increasing institutional capacity and places for technical/scientific training, aligning with 'Access and equity' through expanded opportunities. 'Quality and learning outcomes' is addressed by ensuring academic standards are not compromised. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Financing' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Infrastructure' is not a listed category.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes increasing the number of institutions and available places, which directly relates to expanding physical infrastructure (school buildings) and learning environments (classrooms). WASH, ICT, labs, etc., are not mentioned. The focus on academic standards during expansion supports maintaining quality in physical spaces, but does not explicitly mention other resources like materials or transport.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: To be complete, any education system must, in addition to the means intended to train researchers and engineers, include the creation of courses and diplomas aimed at technicians and skilled workers, whose value is fully recognized within the profession.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes creating courses and diplomas for technicians and skilled workers, which aligns with 'Design and development' (curriculum creation) and 'Content and learning areas' (specific courses). 'Other curriculum-related issue' was considered but excluded due to lack of specific mention. 'Curriculum implementation' and 'evaluation' are not directly addressed. The summary's focus on 'Education orientation' and 'Learner population' supports content design for specific groups.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes creating courses and diplomas for technicians and skilled workers, aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. Mention of skilled workers and technician training relates to 'Lifelong learning and adult education' as it addresses workforce development. 'Education orientation' and 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' are not directly addressed, while 'Policy theme' is too broad.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes skilled workers and technicians, which aligns with adults and lifelong learners. Other categories like children or youth are excluded as the focus is on professional training rather than basic education. 'Learners with disabilities' and 'Indigenous or minority groups' are not mentioned. 'Low-income and disadvantaged groups' is not explicitly referenced.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 24. As far as possible, institutions for the training of technical and scientific personnel should be distributed rationally across the entire territory; in the case of specialized education, the characteristics of the production in the regions where these institutions will be located must be taken into account.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes rational distribution of institutions across territory and alignment with regional production characteristics, which relates to governance and planning. Specialized education's connection to regional production suggests innovation and technology policies. Other categories like curriculum or infrastructure are less directly supported.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes specialized education aligned with regional production needs, fitting 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on technical training. 'Combined' is not selected due to lack of mention of general education integration.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes institutional distribution and alignment with regional production needs, indicating government planning (Government and policy makers) and community-based considerations (Parents and communities). Civil society/NGOs and private sector are less directly mentioned, while curriculum/infrastructure themes are not explicitly addressed.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 25. To the greatest extent possible, the training of technical and scientific personnel must be conducted in the national language, which alone allows the general population and workers to access the essential elements of science and technology.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph emphasizes language as a medium for science and technology content (Content and learning areas) and mandates its use in training (Curriculum implementation). 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or changes. 'Competency-based' is not directly addressed, and other categories lack textual support.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph specifically mentions 'technical and scientific personnel' and emphasizes language access to 'science and technology,' aligning with vocational/technical education. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on practical skills and technical training, not broad academic subjects. 'Combined' is rejected since the paragraph does not mention integration of general and vocational aspects.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 26. Scientific progress, on the one hand, and improvements and innovations in production techniques, on the other hand, require increasing specialization in technical education and consequently in qualifications; however, it is necessary to avoid excessive specialization, especially at the intermediate level of training, both to meet certain pedagogical requirements and to facilitate, later on, the transition from one professional activity to another.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph discusses specialization in technical education and qualifications, aligning with 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. It also mentions balancing specialization with pedagogical requirements and transition between professional activities, supporting 'Combined general and vocational'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on technical and professional training.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph discusses balancing specialization with pedagogical requirements, aligning with 'Pedagogical foundations and theories'. It mentions avoiding excessive specialization to facilitate transitions, which relates to assessment and feedback mechanisms for evaluating educational outcomes. 'Teaching methods and approaches' is less directly supported, and other categories lack explicit mention in the text.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses specialization in technical education and pedagogical requirements, aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. It also mentions scientific progress and production techniques, linking to 'Innovation and technology policies'. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Financing' are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses avoiding excessive specialization to meet pedagogical requirements and facilitate professional transitions, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' (organizing education to meet pedagogical needs). It also implies learning outcomes through the goal of enabling transitions, fitting 'Competency-based and learning outcomes'. 'Content and learning areas' is excluded as the focus is on specialization balance, not content specifics. 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes balancing specialization with pedagogical requirements and facilitating transitions between professional activities, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' for adaptability. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used as the paragraph discusses broader educational orientation and policy implications not fully captured by other categories.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 27. To meet the needs for technical and scientific personnel, it is not sufficient to increase the number of special courses; it is necessary to establish complete sections, departments, or, where appropriate, faculties.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes establishing institutional structures (sections, departments, faculties) to meet technical and scientific personnel needs, aligning with 'Governance and planning' for systemic institutional requirements. 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' is included due to the mention of special courses, though the focus is more on institutional structure than curriculum content. Other categories like 'Education level' and 'Infrastructure & resources' are excluded as they are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes establishing complete sections, departments, or faculties, which directly relates to physical infrastructure (school buildings) and learning environments (classrooms). Categories like ICT, labs, or WASH are not mentioned. 'Other' was excluded as the focus is clearly on institutional structure, not broader resources.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 28. In addition to the specialized training provided by regular study cycles, it is advisable to encourage the establishment, following these cycles, of more advanced specialization studies at the post-secondary and post-graduate levels, as well as the introduction of a technical doctorate.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph mentions post-secondary and post-graduate levels, aligning with ISCED 4 and 5. The technical doctorate directly corresponds to ISCED 8. Earlier levels like primary or secondary are excluded as the focus is on advanced specialization beyond basic education.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes advanced specialization and technical doctorates, which relate to improving educational quality and curriculum depth. 'Quality and learning outcomes' aligns with enhancing educational standards through advanced training. 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' fits as it addresses academic progression frameworks. 'Policy theme' was excluded as the focus is on structural academic recommendations rather than broad policy themes.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 29. In order to improve the training of technical and scientific personnel and to make it more effective, greater integration of practical experience with theory should be pursued by placing more emphasis on practical work within industrial enterprises, laboratories, and research institutes, while also increasing the number of advanced courses in physics and mathematics.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on integrating practical experience with theory and increasing advanced courses in physics/mathematics, which relates to curriculum implementation (structural changes in training) and content/learning areas (subject-specific enhancements). 'Curriculum evaluation and reform' is excluded as there's no mention of assessment or systemic reform. 'Design and development' is less precise here.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph emphasizes physics and mathematics (STEM) and practical experience in industrial settings, aligning with STEM and vocational skills. Other categories like 'Education orientation' are excluded as the focus is on specific subject integration rather than broader educational goals.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes practical experience in industrial settings and advanced courses in physics/mathematics, aligning with vocational/technical (TVET) focus. The mention of both theory and practical work supports 'Combined general and vocational' as it integrates academic and vocational elements.
low_level_thinking.Learning modality: The paragraph discusses integrating practical experience with theory in technical and scientific training, which aligns with formal education structures (e.g., 'industrial enterprises, laboratories, research institutes'). 'Non-formal education' is also relevant as it often involves structured, non-academic learning outside traditional institutions. 'Informal learning' is excluded because the text emphasizes structured integration rather than unstructured experiences. 'Lifelong learning' is not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 30. The shortage of technical and scientific personnel exerts an adverse influence on the recruitment of instructors responsible for training such personnel; serious measures are necessary to attract or retain technicians, engineers, and researchers in education, who may be enticed by more favorable conditions in production, and to direct a sufficient proportion of the best male and female students from secondary or higher education towards teaching and research.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph directly addresses teacher shortages (Teacher supply and shortages), recruitment challenges (Recruitment and hiring), and incentives to retain professionals (Working conditions and salaries). Categories like 'Professional development' and 'Qualification and certification' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Legal status and rights' is indirectly related but not explicitly framed, while 'Teacher other' lacks specific support.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes attracting technicians, engineers, and researchers for education, aligning with vocational/technical (TVET) focus. It also mentions directing students towards teaching and research, supporting combined general and vocational. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the emphasis is on technical workforce needs.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes the need for technical personnel in education and attracting/retaining educators, directly linking to 'Teachers and educators'. 'Government and policy makers' is relevant due to the mention of systemic policies and measures required to address workforce issues. Other categories like 'Private sector and industry' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Learners and students' are not the focus here.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses directing students towards teaching/research, aligning with 'Curriculum implementation' through workforce alignment. 'Competency-based and learning outcomes' is supported by the focus on training technical personnel and ensuring sufficient skilled educators. 'Design and development' is excluded as the text focuses on implementation, not curriculum design. 'Content and learning areas' is less precise than 'Competency-based' which directly relates to skill development.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 31. Among the measures that could help increase the number of qualified teachers, particularly those who dedicate themselves solely to teaching, are provisions aimed at broadening recruitment and ensuring the training of staff for secondary and higher education, the adoption of adequate remuneration, increased recognition of the value and importance of the teacher’s work, and the improvement of social security for the teaching profession; aside from permanently employed personnel, it may be useful to entrust part-time teaching duties to members of the production and research sectors.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph discusses recruitment (part-time roles, expanding recruitment), initial training (training of staff), working conditions (remuneration, social security), and teacher supply (shortages addressed by recruitment measures). 'Professional development' is excluded as it refers to ongoing training, not initial training. 'Qualification and certification' is not explicitly mentioned. 'Legal status and rights' is not directly addressed. 'Teacher evaluation and performance' and 'Teacher other' are not relevant here.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on teacher recruitment, training, remuneration, and social security, directly aligning with 'Teachers and workforce policies'. 'Access and equity' applies due to emphasis on expanding recruitment and improving conditions. 'Financing and resource mobilization' is relevant to adequate remuneration and social security. Other categories like 'Curriculum' or 'Innovation' are not mentioned, and 'Education level' remains contested per the summary.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph mentions 'provisions aimed at broadening recruitment' and 'permanent employment' which align with public sector initiatives. The reference to part-time roles entrusted to 'production and research sectors' suggests collaboration beyond public alone, supporting PPP. Private categories are excluded as the focus remains on public-led measures and partnerships.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph focuses on teacher recruitment, training, remuneration, and social security, directly relating to 'Teachers and educators'. 'Government and policy makers' is included due to references to policy measures and systemic changes. Other categories like 'Private sector and industry' are excluded as the part-time roles mentioned are not tied to private sector involvement.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training and professional development, aligning with 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' through skill-building and adaptability. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is used as the focus on teacher recruitment, remuneration, and social security doesn't fit neatly into existing categories. Categories like 'Human rights and equality' or 'Inclusive education' are excluded as the text doesn't explicitly address rights frameworks or special needs.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 33. In order to ensure the training of the technical and scientific personnel required by modern life, it is desirable that institutions of higher technical education and universities have access to advanced scientific working facilities and research resources that attract teaching staff, students, and graduate researchers, through the establishment of laboratories equipped with sophisticated scientific and technical equipment, as well as scientific and technical libraries.

'Private sector and industry' is included as the text references advanced equipment and resources that may involve industry collaboration. 'Government and policy makers' is excluded as there's no direct mention of policy or governance. 'Civil society and NGOs' and 'International organizations and donors' are not explicitly referenced.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 34. In order to increase, from primary school onwards, students' interest in technical and scientific studies, it is appropriate to use active methods designed to develop in them an experimental mindset.

'Vocational and technical skills' is excluded as the focus is on scientific inquiry rather than vocational training.

'Technology-supported pedagogy' is excluded as no technology reference is present. 'Assessment and feedback' is not directly addressed. 'Professional learning communities' and 'Other pedagogy related issue' lack explicit support.

paragraph: 35. The rightful place must be reserved for mathematics and the sciences in the curricula of both primary and secondary schools, and adequate time must also be devoted to exercises and practical work, laboratory work, manual and agricultural activities, etc.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 36. The increasingly rapid development of science and technology requires that the equipment used in schools be adequate and kept up to date; there should be constant collaboration between teachers and scientists in their research to design and develop the equipment and — 204 — facilities necessary for the understanding of basic scientific concepts in secondary and higher education; it should be noted that mass communication technologies (radio, television, cinema, etc.) can greatly contribute to informing young people and stimulating their interest in scientific and technical careers.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions secondary and higher education in the context of scientific concepts and equipment, aligning with 'Secondary education unspecified (ISCED 2, 3)' and 'Higher education unspecified (ISCED 5, 6, 7, 8)'. low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions mass communication technologies (ICT) and equipment for scientific concepts, supporting 'ICT and digital infrastructure'. 'Teaching and learning materials' covers equipment and facilities for education. 'Laboratories and libraries' aligns with facilities for scientific understanding. 'School buildings' and 'WASH' are not explicitly mentioned. 'Education level' is contextually relevant but not a sub-label category.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph explicitly mentions science and technology education, equipment, and facilities for secondary and higher education, directly supporting 'Science and technology (STEM)' and 'Education level'. low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes science and technology education, aligning with 'Digital and ICT literacy' through mentions of equipment, facilities, and mass communication technologies. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits due to the focus on preparing students for scientific careers and fostering interest. TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 37. It is highly advantageous to establish, within primary and post-primary education, a school guidance service operating in connection with the vocational guidance service, as these two services are expected to play an increasingly important role in the identification and selection of future technical and scientific cadres; where proper guidance services do not exist, teachers should have the opportunity to familiarize themselves with techniques that can be successfully used to ensure the educational and vocational guidance of their students.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'primary and post-primary education,' directly aligning with 'Primary education (ISCED 1)' and 'Post-secondary non-tertiary (ISCED 4).' 'Post-primary' is classified under ISCED 4, which includes post-secondary non-tertiary education. Other categories like secondary education or tertiary levels are not explicitly referenced, and the focus on guidance services aligns with cross-cutting themes rather than specific subject domains.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph explicitly mentions vocational guidance services and technical/scientific cadres, supporting 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. It also references primary/post-primary education and guidance services bridging academic and vocational roles, justifying 'Combined general and vocational'. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the focus is on vocational integration, not pure academic orientation.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph discusses establishing guidance services in primary/post-primary education (Initial education and training) and teachers needing to familiarize themselves with techniques for guidance (Professional development). Legal status, recruitment, and working conditions are not addressed. Teacher supply and evaluation are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes vocational and technical guidance within education, aligning with curriculum policies (e.g., 'vocational guidance service', 'technical and scientific cadres'). It also mentions teacher training opportunities ('teachers should have the opportunity to familiarize themselves'), supporting teachers and workforce policies. Other categories like governance or financing are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes guidance services for identifying and selecting technical/scientific cadres, aligning with life skills for career development. 'Inclusive education' is implied by addressing gaps in guidance services, ensuring equitable access. Other categories like citizenship or digital literacy are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: 38. To guide students and parents in their choice of studies and professions, it would be appropriate to prepare guides addressing secondary and higher education, the requirements for undertaking them, and the career opportunities to which they lead.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: At a time when efforts are being made to intensify the specialized training of future technical and scientific executives, it is essential to accord the necessary importance, in the preparation of these executives, to general education subjects and to all that can contribute to cultivating the mind.

'Cross-cutting themes' and 'Education orientation' are not selected due to lack of explicit focus on holistic skills or broader educational philosophy.

TITLE: LES MESURES DESTINÉES A FACILITER LE RECRUTEMENT ET LA FORMATION DES CADRES TECHNIQUES ET SCIENTIFIQUES
paragraph: It is important to provide workers who are already practicing a profession with all possible support (notably in the form of paid internships) so that they can undertake technical or scientific studies at the secondary or higher level, enabling them to access the functions assigned to managerial staff.
